seoTitle: Add HubSpot to Outlook: Sales Add-in Setup
metaDescription: Install the HubSpot Sales add-in in Outlook to log and track emails, then use the sidebar to update deal stages without leaving your inbox.
intro: The HubSpot Sales add-in puts your CRM directly inside Outlook, so you can log emails and track opens without switching windows. This guide covers finding the add-in in Microsoft's marketplace, authenticating it with your HubSpot account, and turning on tracking for your outgoing messages. It ends with using the sidebar to create contacts or update deals from within an email.

steps.heading: Open the Outlook add-ins store
steps.body: In Outlook, click the Get Add-ins or Apps component to browse available extensions.

steps.heading: Find the HubSpot Sales plugin
steps.body: Search for HubSpot in the marketplace and select the official Sales plugin.

steps.heading: Install the add-in
steps.body: Click Add or Install to inject the HubSpot framework into your Outlook interface.

steps.heading: Sign in and authenticate
steps.body: Open a compose window, click the HubSpot icon, and pass the OAuth screen to select your target portal.

steps.heading: Turn on tracking
steps.body: Toggle Log Email and Track Email so HubSpot monitors opens and logs interactions automatically.

steps.heading: Use the sidebar in your inbox
steps.body: Send a test message and check that contact profiles sync inside your CRM dashboard.

faq.question: Does the HubSpot Outlook add-in work with the web version?
faq.answer: It's designed to work with Outlook clients that support Microsoft add-ins, which generally includes the web version.

faq.question: Will HubSpot automatically log every email I send?
faq.answer: Only emails you choose to log or track will sync, based on the toggle settings in your compose window.

faq.question: Can I update deal stages directly from Outlook?
faq.answer: Yes, the persistent HubSpot sidebar lets you create contacts or update deal stages without leaving your email.

faq.question: Do I need a HubSpot account to use the add-in?
faq.answer: Yes, you'll need to authenticate with an existing HubSpot portal during setup.
